learning_outcomes:
ENGE 1.  |  Major Programme:
Through the course of their studies, graduates will have
learnt:
(1)  |  to read, understand, and appreciate literatures in
English, specifically in the major genres of poetry, fiction, and drama; (K/S/V)
(2)  |  to understand and apply the systematic study of language; (K/S)
(3)  |  to develop an understanding and appreciation of the local
and global dimensions of English, including multilingualism and
multiculturalism; (K/V)
(4)  |  to produce creative and scholarly work in high quality
written English, using a variety of styles, both formal and informal,
academic and nonacademic; (K/S)
(5)  |  to communicate effectively in spoken English in a manner
appropriate to the communicative and cultural context, including making
effective oral presentations using appropriate technology; and (S/V)
(6)  |  to continue building upon their knowledge and skills
after graduation, with an interest in learning as a lifelong process. (K/S/V)
K = Knowledge outcomes S = Skills outcomes V =
Values & attitude outcomes
2.  |  English
and History Double Major Programme:
Upon completing the Programme, students will have
acquired:
(1)  |  command of appropriate academic and creative English
language skills; (K)
(2)  |  progress in humanities research, particularly linguistic
and historical research; (K)
(3)  |  deepened understanding of both English and Chinese
culture and history; (K)
(4)  |  critical and creative thinking; (S)
(5)  |  communication skills and computer literacy; (S)
(6)  |  self-managed learning, adaptability and problem solving
skills; (S)
(7)  |  ability to appreciate global cultural diversity,
exchanges and mutual dependence; (V)
(8)  |  ability to value and appreciate the beauty of language
and culture and the pleasure of humanities research; and (V)
(9)  |  ability to consolidate cosmopolitan identity and cultural
openness of Hong Kong. (V)
K = Knowledge outcomes S = Skills outcomes V =
Values & attitude outcomes
3.  |  Minor Programme:
(i)  |  English
Upon completion of the Minor
Programme, students will have demonstrated basic understanding of fundamental
issues related to the subject, acquired knowledge of some aspects of the
subject with sufficient exposure and at a suitable level to allow further
study in this subject or related subjects or to pursue a related career.
(ii)  |  Creative Writing in English
Upon
completion of the Minor Programme, students will have acquired the following
outcomes:
(a)  |  Community
engagement – students and graduates equipped with creative writing skills
will be able to contribute meaningfully to the cultural life of Hong Kong.
They will be able to organize and participate in English language literary
readings and creative writing workshops and enhance the cultural life of Hong
Kong;
(b)  |  Educational
needs – graduates of the course who become teachers will be equipped to teach
creative writing to their students and assess creative writing in English in
schools; and
(c)  |  Intrapersonal
needs – as creative writing deals with personal feelings that may not be
easily expressed in analytical academic writings, it helps students in their
search for meaning in their lives.

who have completed the courses ENGE1310, 1320 and 3100 will be eligible

are required to apply for the exemption. When exemption from a particular
course is recognized, students can only be exempted from the course but
not the units.

courses at 2000 and above level will be included in the calculation of
Major GPA for honours classification.
